parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether a Neptune DB cluster publishes audit logs to Amazon CloudWatch Logs. The control fails if a Neptune DB cluster doesn't publish audit logs to CloudWatch Logs. `EnableCloudWatchLogsExport` should be set to Audit.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Neptune DB clusters should publish audit logs to CloudWatch Logs

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether a Neptune manual DB cluster snapshot is public. The control fails if a Neptune manual DB cluster snapshot is public.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Neptune DB cluster snapshots should not be public

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks if a Neptune DB cluster has deletion protection enabled. The control fails if a Neptune DB cluster doesn't have deletion protection enabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Neptune DB clusters should have deletion protection enabled

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether a Neptune DB cluster has automated backups enabled, and a backup retention period greater than or equal to the specified time frame. The control fails if backups aren't enabled for the Neptune DB cluster, or if the retention period is less than the specified time frame. Unless you provide a custom parameter value for the backup retention period, Security Hub uses a default value of 7 days.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Neptune DB clusters should have automated backups enabled

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether a Neptune DB cluster snapshot is encrypted at rest. The control fails if a Neptune DB cluster isn't encrypted at rest.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Neptune DB cluster snapshots should be encrypted at rest

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks if a Neptune DB cluster has IAM database authentication enabled. The control fails if IAM database authentication isn't enabled for a Neptune DB cluster.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Neptune DB clusters should have IAM database authentication enabled

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks if a Neptune DB cluster is configured to copy all tags to snapshots when the snapshots are created. The control fails if a Neptune DB cluster isn't configured to copy tags to snapshots.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Neptune DB clusters should be configured to copy tags to snapshots

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks if an Amazon Neptune DB cluster has read-replica instances in multiple Availability Zones (AZs). The control fails if the cluster is deployed in only one AZ.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Neptune DB clusters should be deployed across multiple Availability Zones

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control evaluates whether a firewall managed through AWS Network Firewall is deployed across multiple Availability Zones (AZs). The control fails if a firewall is deployed in only one AZ.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Network Firewall firewalls should be deployed across multiple Availability Zones

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether subnet change protection is enabled for an AWS Network Firewall firewall. The control fails if subnet change protection isn't enabled for the firewall.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Network Firewall firewalls should have subnet change protection enabled

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether logging is enabled for an AWS Network Firewall firewall. The control fails if logging isn't enabled for at least one log type or if the logging destination doesn't exist.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Network Firewall logging should be enabled

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether a Network Firewall policy has any stateful or stateless rule groups associated. This control fails if stateless or stateful rule groups are not assigned.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Network Firewall policies should have at least one rule group associated

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether the default stateless action for full packets for a Network Firewall policy is drop or forward. The control passes if Drop or Forward is selected, and fails if Pass is selected.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The default stateless action for Network Firewall policies should be drop or forward for full packets

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether the default stateless action for fragmented packets for a Network Firewall policy is drop or forward. The control passes if Drop or Forward is selected, and fails if Pass is selected.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The default stateless action for Network Firewall policies should be drop or forward for fragmented packets

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks if a Stateless Network Firewall Rule Group contains rules. The rule will fail if there are no rules in a Stateless Network Firewall Rule Group.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Stateless network firewall rule group should not be empty

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether an AWS Network Firewall firewall has deletion protection enabled. The control fails if deletion protection isn't enabled for a firewall.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Network Firewall firewalls should have deletion protection enabled

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether Amazon OpenSearch domains have encryption-at-rest configuration enabled. The check fails if encryption at rest is not enabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: OpenSearch domains should have encryption at rest enabled

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether an Amazon OpenSearch domain is configured with at least three dedicated primary nodes. This control fails if the domain has fewer than three dedicated primary nodes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: OpenSearch domains should have at least three dedicated primary nodes

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether an OpenSearch domain is publicly accessible. The control fails if an OpenSearch domain is publicly accessible. It does not evaluate the VPC subnet routing configuration to determine public access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: OpenSearch domains should not be publicly accessible

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether Amazon OpenSearch domains have node-to-node encryption enabled. This control fails if node-to-node encryption is disabled on the domain.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: OpenSearch domains should encrypt data sent between nodes

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether Amazon OpenSearch domains are configured to send error logs to CloudWatch Logs. This control fails if error logging to CloudWatch is not enabled for a domain.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: OpenSearch domain error logging to CloudWatch Logs should be enabled

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether Amazon OpenSearch Service domains have audit logging enabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: OpenSearch domains should have audit logging enabled

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether Amazon OpenSearch Service domains are configured with at least three data nodes and "zoneAwarenessEnabled" is true.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: OpenSearch domains should have at least three data nodes

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: For information on how to correct this issue, consult the AWS Security Hub controls documentation.

parts.name: overview
parts.prose: This control checks whether Amazon OpenSearch domains have fine-grained access control enabled. This control fails if the fine-grained access control is not enabled.

parts.prose: This control checks whether an AWS Private CA certificate authority has tags with the specific keys defined in the parameter requiredKeyTags. System tags, which are automatically applied and begin with aws:, are ignored.

parts.name: statement
